description_long: This is a roughly cylindrical, irregularly shaped stone with a coarse, granular texture and a mottled appearance. The surface is composed of small, visible pebbles and mineral fragments embedded in a matrix, giving it a speckled look with shades of gray, brown, and tan. One side features a small, circular label with faded writing, suggesting it may have been cataloged or collected for study. The stone's edges are uneven and jagged, with no smooth or polished surfaces, indicating it has not been artificially shaped. Its overall form is blocky and robust, making it suitable as a natural obstacle or barrier in an urban environment. The stone appears dense and solid, with no visible cavities or openings.
description: A coarse, irregularly shaped stone with a granular, speckled surface and a small label on one side. Its blocky form and rough texture make it a natural obstacle or barrier.
description_view_0: This view shows the broadest face of the stone, highlighting its granular texture and the small, circular label affixed to the surface.
description_view_1: The stone appears narrower from this angle, emphasizing its irregular, elongated profile and rough, jagged edges.
description_view_2: This side displays a relatively flat, wide surface with a consistent speckled pattern and no visible markings or labels.
description_view_3: The stone is seen from an oblique angle, showing both the rough side and part of the broader face, with the granular texture prominent.
attribute_placement: set as a standalone feature or used as a boundary marker
attribute_pattern: Mottled, speckled pattern from embedded pebbles and mineral grains; irregular and non-repetitive.
attribute_weathering: Rough and unpolished with minor surface pitting, indicating moderate natural weathering.
attribute_integration_with_surroundings: Contrasts with smooth man-made surfaces but can blend with other natural stones or landscaping elements.
